name: Aspirin
agent_summary: This molecule exhibits excellent drug-likeness based on physicochemical parameters, passing Lipinski rules. It has good size, moderate lipophilicity, and low rotatable bonds.
agent_liabilities: Potential metabolic instability due to the ester linkage., Carboxylic acid moiety can lead to ionization issues and potential GI irritation., Moderate QED score suggests room for further optimization of overall drug-likeness.
agent_optimization: Explore bioisosteric replacement of the ester to enhance metabolic stability., Investigate modifications or prodrug strategies for the carboxylic acid to modulate acidity and improve permeability., Explore SAR around the aromatic ring to fine-tune potency and ADMET profiles., Synthesize analogs to optimize physicochemical properties and improve the QED score.

name: Ibuprofen
agent_summary: Ibuprofen exhibits excellent drug-like properties with no Lipinski violations and a high QED score. Its liabilities are primarily class-related, impacting GI, renal, and cardiovascular systems.
agent_liabilities: GI irritation/ulceration (due to non-selective COX inhibition), Increased cardiovascular risk with chronic use, Potential for renal toxicity, especially in susceptible individuals, Non-selective cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition
agent_optimization: Enhance COX-2 selectivity to mitigate gastrointestinal side effects, Investigate prodrug approaches for targeted delivery or improved safety profile, Explore single (S)-enantiomer to optimize efficacy and potentially reduce metabolic burden, Introduce structural features to minimize cardiovascular liabilities
